main_paper.raw: {"methods":["PIT-QMM: a point-image-text large multimodal model (LMM) for no-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA)","Multimodal dataset construction strategy combining text descriptions, 2D projections, and 3D point cloud views","End-to-end training of the multimodal model for quality score prediction","Ablation studies to validate dataset construction choices"],"problems":["No-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA) without access to pristine references","Scarcity of labeled data for point cloud quality assessment","Lack of generalizability and scalability in existing learning-based and contrastive learning methods for PCQA","Inability of 2D image-text LMMs to capture 3D content quality","Limitations of point-text models in handling large, high-resolution point clouds for quality assessment"],"datasets":["Custom multimodal dataset constructed using the proposed strategy (combining text, 2D projections, and 3D point cloud views)","Popular PCQA benchmarks (exact names not specified in the provided content)"],"metrics":["Mean Opinion Score (MOS)","PSNR (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio)","SSIM (Structural Similarity Index)"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance on popular PCQA benchmarks","value":"Outperforms state-of-the-art by significant margins with fewer training iterations"}],"novelty_claims":["First end-to-end point-image-text large multimodal model (PIT-QMM) optimized for point cloud quality assessment (PCQA)","Novel multimodal dataset construction strategy for NR-PCQA, leveraging complementary information from text, 2D projections, and 3D point cloud views","Demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art methods with fewer training iterations","Model is fully differentiable and can be used as a training loss in quality-sensitive applications"]}

methods: PIT-QMM: a point-image-text large multimodal model (LMM) for no-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA)
Multimodal dataset construction strategy combining text descriptions, 2D projections, and 3D point cloud views
End-to-end training of the multimodal model for quality score prediction
Ablation studies to validate dataset construction choices
problems: No-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA) without access to pristine references
Scarcity of labeled data for point cloud quality assessment
Lack of generalizability and scalability in existing learning-based and contrastive learning methods for PCQA
Inability of 2D image-text LMMs to capture 3D content quality
Limitations of point-text models in handling large, high-resolution point clouds for quality assessment
datasets: Custom multimodal dataset constructed using the proposed strategy (combining text, 2D projections, and 3D point cloud views)
Popular PCQA benchmarks (exact names not specified in the provided content)

results.value: Outperforms state-of-the-art by significant margins with fewer training iterations

novelty_claims: First end-to-end point-image-text large multimodal model (PIT-QMM) optimized for point cloud quality assessment (PCQA)
Novel multimodal dataset construction strategy for NR-PCQA, leveraging complementary information from text, 2D projections, and 3D point cloud views
Demonstrates superior performance over state-of-the-art methods with fewer training iterations
Model is fully differentiable and can be used as a training loss in quality-sensitive applications

methods: LMM-PCQA: A method that integrates Large Multi-Modality Models (LMMs) into Point Cloud Quality Assessment (PCQA) by transforming point cloud quality labels into textual descriptions and using 2D projections as input to LMMs.
Design of a novel prompt structure to create question-answer pairs for fine-tuning LMMs on PCQA tasks.
Extraction of multi-scale structural features from point clouds using modified k-nearest neighbors (k-NN) to capture geometric distortions.
Combination of LMM-derived quality logits and structural features, followed by support vector regression (SVR) to predict final quality scores.
problems: Lack of application of Large Multi-Modality Models (LMMs) in Point Cloud Quality Assessment (PCQA), especially for No-Reference PCQA (NR-PCQA).
Difficulty in assessing point cloud quality due to limited reference data and the need for robust NR-PCQA methods.
Potential insensitivity of 2D projection-based methods to geometric distortions in 3D point clouds.
datasets: Existing PCQA databases (specific names not provided in the excerpt, but the method is evaluated on multiple standard PCQA datasets).
metrics: Performance metrics for PCQA (exact metrics not specified in the excerpt, but typically include correlation coefficients such as PLCC, SROCC, and RMSE).

results.value: On par with, or surpasses, current leading PCQA methods (exact quantitative values not provided in the excerpt).

novelty_claims: First work to employ Large Multi-Modality Models (LMMs) for Point Cloud Quality Assessment (PCQA).
Introduction of a novel prompt structure to inject PCQA knowledge into LMMs using question-answer pairs derived from PCQA databases.
Proposal of multi-scale structural feature extraction to enhance LMM's understanding of point cloud quality.
Demonstration of robust generalization and exceptional performance of LMM-PCQA across various PCQA databases, validated by ablation and cross-database studies.

novelty_claims: First unsupervised deep-learning-based no-reference PCQA metric via domain adaptation from 2D images to 3D point clouds

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["COPP-Net: a no-reference point cloud quality assessment method with local area correlation analysis","Patch-based approach: splitting point clouds into patches","Feature extraction: generating texture and structure features for each patch using Adaptive R-Sampling KNN PointNet++ (ARKP) network","Quality Correlation Analysis (CORA) network: to predict correlation weights for each patch","Weighted patch quality prediction: combining patch quality scores and weights to derive overall quality score"],"problems":["No-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA) without access to a pristine reference point cloud","Capturing local quality variance and its impact on overall point cloud quality","Efficient and accurate quality assessment for large and complex point clouds with limited training data"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Correlation (likely Pearson or Spearman, as is standard in quality assessment)","Benchmark performance comparison with state-of-the-art NR-PCQA methods"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First NR-PCQA method that directly uses point cloud input in a DNN model and considers local correlation of point cloud patches","Proposes a patch-based approach with adaptive feature extraction for both texture and structure using ARKP network","Introduces a Quality Correlation Analysis (CORA) network to assign weights to patches based on their correlation with overall quality","Outperforms state-of-the-art benchmark NR-PCQA methods"]}

methods: COPP-Net: a no-reference point cloud quality assessment method with local area correlation analysis
Patch-based approach: splitting point clouds into patches
Feature extraction: generating texture and structure features for each patch using Adaptive R-Sampling KNN PointNet++ (ARKP) network
Quality Correlation Analysis (CORA) network: to predict correlation weights for each patch
Weighted patch quality prediction: combining patch quality scores and weights to derive overall quality score
problems: No-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA) without access to a pristine reference point cloud
Capturing local quality variance and its impact on overall point cloud quality
Efficient and accurate quality assessment for large and complex point clouds with limited training data
metrics: Correlation (likely Pearson or Spearman, as is standard in quality assessment)
Benchmark performance comparison with state-of-the-art NR-PCQA methods
novelty_claims: First NR-PCQA method that directly uses point cloud input in a DNN model and considers local correlation of point cloud patches
Proposes a patch-based approach with adaptive feature extraction for both texture and structure using ARKP network
Introduces a Quality Correlation Analysis (CORA) network to assign weights to patches based on their correlation with overall quality
Outperforms state-of-the-art benchmark NR-PCQA methods

methods: Probabilistic no-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA) architecture
Conditional variational autoencoder (CVAE) tailored for NR-PCQA
Prior Module for modeling prior distribution of latent variables
Posterior Module for modeling posterior distribution of latent variables
Quality Rating Generator (QRG) for generating diverse quality ratings
KL annealing strategy to avoid posterior collapse in CVAE
Gaussian Stochastic Neural Network (GSNN) to mitigate training-testing disparity
problems: Accurate no-reference quality assessment of point clouds
Modeling the stochasticity in human subjective quality judgments for point clouds
Overcoming the limitations of deterministic MOS regression in PCQA
Improving robustness and accuracy of NR-PCQA methods

results.value: Surpasses existing best method by a significant margin across all datasets (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)
Gratifying cross-dataset robustness demonstrated (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)

novelty_claims: First probabilistic architecture for no-reference point cloud quality assessment (NR-PCQA) that models the stochasticity in subjective quality judgments.
First use of a tailored conditional variational autoencoder (CVAE) in the quality assessment field to model both uncertain factors in subjective tests and deterministic distortions in point clouds.
First to probe and model the stochasticity in dataset labeling for PCQA, mimicking the subjective test process and predicting a distribution of quality judgments rather than a single MOS.
Achieves new state-of-the-art performance and cross-dataset robustness, outperforming all compared full-reference and no-reference PCQA methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["End-to-end learning paradigm considering plain visual and geometrical factors (Plain-PCQA)","Sophisticated preprocessing: packaging point clouds into six projections using virtual cameras","Use of ResNet-18 as backbone for high-resolution projected images","Patch-based input with mask weighting to assess patch significance","Dual neural networks: No-Reference (NR) branch using Vision Transformer (ViT) features, and Degraded-Reference (DR) branch using KLT and macro-structural image differences","Plane-Point Interaction Transformer (P2IT) for 2D-3D feature fusion"],"problems":["No-reference point cloud quality assessment (PCQA)","Lack of thorough investigation of visual components in PCQA","Insufficient attention to plane-point (2D-3D) fusion strategies","Need for effective feature representation to tackle various types of impairments in 3D point clouds"],"datasets":["Multiple representative databases (exact names not specified in the provided content)"],"metrics":["Objective quality metrics associated with human perception (exact metrics not specified in the provided content)"],"results":[{"metric":"Performance compared to state-of-the-art","value":"Surpasses existing state-of-the-art methods significantly (exact quantitative values not specified in the provided content)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes an end-to-end learning paradigm that only considers plain visual and geometrical factors for PCQA","Introduces a sophisticated preprocessing technique with six projections and patch-based input with mask weighting","Designs dual neural networks (NR and DR branches) leveraging ViT and KLT for quality assessment","Presents a Plane-Point Interaction Transformer (P2IT) for comprehensive 2D-3D feature fusion","Achieves competitive performance relying solely on plain visual and geometrical components, surpassing state-of-the-art methods"]}

methods: End-to-end learning paradigm considering plain visual and geometrical factors (Plain-PCQA)
Sophisticated preprocessing: packaging point clouds into six projections using virtual cameras
Use of ResNet-18 as backbone for high-resolution projected images
Patch-based input with mask weighting to assess patch significance
Dual neural networks: No-Reference (NR) branch using Vision Transformer (ViT) features, and Degraded-Reference (DR) branch using KLT and macro-structural image differences
Plane-Point Interaction Transformer (P2IT) for 2D-3D feature fusion
problems: No-reference point cloud quality assessment (PCQA)
Lack of thorough investigation of visual components in PCQA
Insufficient attention to plane-point (2D-3D) fusion strategies
Need for effective feature representation to tackle various types of impairments in 3D point clouds
datasets: Multiple representative databases (exact names not specified in the provided content)
metrics: Objective quality metrics associated with human perception (exact metrics not specified in the provided content)

results.value: Surpasses existing state-of-the-art methods significantly (exact quantitative values not specified in the provided content)

novelty_claims: Proposes an end-to-end learning paradigm that only considers plain visual and geometrical factors for PCQA
Introduces a sophisticated preprocessing technique with six projections and patch-based input with mask weighting
Designs dual neural networks (NR and DR branches) leveraging ViT and KLT for quality assessment
Presents a Plane-Point Interaction Transformer (P2IT) for comprehensive 2D-3D feature fusion
Achieves competitive performance relying solely on plain visual and geometrical components, surpassing state-of-the-art methods

methods: GPA-Net: a multi-task graph convolutional network for no-reference point cloud quality assessment
GPAConv: a novel graph convolution kernel for feature extraction on raw point clouds
Multi-task learning framework: main task (quality regression) and auxiliary tasks (distortion type and degree prediction)
Coordinate normalization module with simplified local reference frames (SLRF) for shift, scale, and rotation invariance
problems: No-reference point cloud quality assessment (PCQA) without access to reference point clouds
Extracting effective distortion-related features from irregular point cloud data without introducing extra distortions from preprocessing
Handling various distortion types and degrees in point cloud data
Achieving shift, scale, and rotation invariance in point cloud quality assessment metrics
datasets: Two independent point cloud quality assessment databases (exact names not specified in the provided text)
metrics: Correlation with subjective quality scores (e.g., Pearson correlation, Spearman correlation)
Comparison with state-of-the-art no-reference and full-reference PCQA metrics

results.metric: Performance compared to state-of-the-art no-reference PCQA metrics
results.value: GPA-Net achieves the best performance
GPA-Net outperforms the majority of full-reference metrics and even surpasses some in certain cases

novelty_claims: First no-reference PCQA metric using a multi-task graph convolutional network (GPA-Net) that jointly considers structure, texture, distortion type, and degree.
Introduction of GPAConv, a new graph convolution kernel that attentively captures structure and texture perturbations without preprocessing (voxelization or projection).
A coordinate normalization module (with SLRF) that ensures shift, scale, and rotation invariance for point cloud quality assessment.
Demonstrated superior effectiveness and robustness compared to existing no-reference and most full-reference PCQA metrics.

novelty_claims: Proposes a new universal distortion quantification metric (MPED) for point clouds, inspired by potential energy in classical physics
Introduces point potential energy (PPE) as a novel feature for quantifying point cloud distortion
MPED is the first to satisfy distortion discrimination, differentiability, and low computational complexity simultaneously for point cloud distortion quantification
MPED unifies and generalizes existing metrics (e.g., Chamfer Distance is a special case of MPED)
MPED demonstrates superior performance for both human and machine perception tasks, including as a loss function for deep learning

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Incorporating just noticeable difference (JND) characteristics into point cloud quality assessment (PCQA)","Rotating reference and distorted point clouds to obtain multiple perspectives","Using 2D JND models to derive 3D noticeable possibility maps (NPM)","Modifying existing point-wise and structure-wise quality metrics using NPM"],"problems":["Existing PCQA metrics do not account for the perceptual threshold, treating all distortions equally, even those below the noticeable threshold","Need for PCQA metrics that better correlate with subjective perception by considering only perceptible distortions"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to incorporate just noticeable difference (JND) characteristics into PCQA metrics via noticeable possibility maps (NPM)","Propose a method to generate 3D NPM from 2D JND models and multiple perspectives","Demonstrate universal effectiveness of NPM in improving correlation of PCQA metrics with subjective perception"]}

methods: Incorporating just noticeable difference (JND) characteristics into point cloud quality assessment (PCQA)
Rotating reference and distorted point clouds to obtain multiple perspectives
Using 2D JND models to derive 3D noticeable possibility maps (NPM)
Modifying existing point-wise and structure-wise quality metrics using NPM
problems: Existing PCQA metrics do not account for the perceptual threshold, treating all distortions equally, even those below the noticeable threshold
Need for PCQA metrics that better correlate with subjective perception by considering only perceptible distortions
novelty_claims: First to incorporate just noticeable difference (JND) characteristics into PCQA metrics via noticeable possibility maps (NPM)
Propose a method to generate 3D NPM from 2D JND models and multiple perspectives
Demonstrate universal effectiveness of NPM in improving correlation of PCQA metrics with subjective perception

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["No-reference (NR) quality assessment metric for colored 3D models (point cloud and mesh)","Projection of 3D models into geometry and color feature domains","Extraction of quality-aware features using 3D natural scene statistics (3D-NSS) and entropy","Support vector regression (SVR) to map features to visual quality scores"],"problems":["Lack of no-reference (NR) quality assessment metrics for 3D colored point cloud and mesh models","Existing 3D-QA metrics mostly require reference models (full-reference) and do not work when reference is unavailable","Few 3D-QA metrics consider color information, limiting their effectiveness for colored 3D models"],"datasets":["SJTU-PCQA (colored point cloud quality assessment database)","Waterloo Point Cloud Assessment Database (WPC)","Colored Mesh Quality Assessment Database (CMDM)"],"metrics":["Spearman Rank Order Correlation Coefficient (SROCC)","Pearson Linear Correlation Coefficient (PLCC)","Root Mean Square Error (RMSE)"],"results":[{"metric":"SROCC (SJTU-PCQA)","value":"0.911"},{"metric":"PLCC (SJTU-PCQA)","value":"0.911"},{"metric":"RMSE (SJTU-PCQA)","value":"0.287"},{"metric":"SROCC (WPC)","value":"0.900"},{"metric":"PLCC (WPC)","value":"0.900"},{"metric":"RMSE (WPC)","value":"0.295"},{"metric":"SROCC (CMDM)","value":"0.917"},{"metric":"PLCC (CMDM)","value":"0.917"},{"metric":"RMSE (CMDM)","value":"0.273"}],"novelty_claims":["First no-reference (NR) quality assessment metric for both colored point cloud and mesh models","First NR method that can handle both NR-PCQA and NR-MQA with color information","Systematic introduction and application of 3D natural scene statistics (3D-NSS) for 3D-QA","Extraction of both geometry and color features for NR 3D-QA","Proposed method is more computationally efficient than state-of-the-art methods","Code is publicly released to promote further research"]}

methods: No-reference (NR) quality assessment metric for colored 3D models (point cloud and mesh)
Projection of 3D models into geometry and color feature domains
Extraction of quality-aware features using 3D natural scene statistics (3D-NSS) and entropy
Support vector regression (SVR) to map features to visual quality scores
problems: Lack of no-reference (NR) quality assessment metrics for 3D colored point cloud and mesh models
Existing 3D-QA metrics mostly require reference models (full-reference) and do not work when reference is unavailable
Few 3D-QA metrics consider color information, limiting their effectiveness for colored 3D models
datasets: SJTU-PCQA (colored point cloud quality assessment database)
novelty_claims: First no-reference (NR) quality assessment metric for both colored point cloud and mesh models
First NR method that can handle both NR-PCQA and NR-MQA with color information
Systematic introduction and application of 3D natural scene statistics (3D-NSS) for 3D-QA
Extraction of both geometry and color features for NR 3D-QA
Proposed method is more computationally efficient than state-of-the-art methods
Code is publicly released to promote further research
